sn15.13:1.1: Now on that occasion the Blessed One was staying near Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Forest, the Squirrels’ Sanctuary.
sn15.13:1.2: Then thirty monks from Pāva—all wilderness dwellers, all alms-goers, all cast-off rag wearers, all triple-robe wearers, all still with fetters, went to the Blessed One and, on arrival, having bowed down to him, sat to one side.
sn15.13:1.3: Then the thought occurred to the Blessed One,
sn15.13:1.4: “These thirty monks from Pāva… are all still with fetters.
sn15.13:1.5: What if I were to teach them the Dhamma in such a way that in this very sitting their minds, through lack of clinging, would be released from effluents?”
sn15.13:2.1: “From an inconceivable beginning comes the wandering-on.
sn15.13:2.2: A beginning point is not discernible, though beings hindered by ignorance and fettered by craving are transmigrating & wandering on.
sn15.13:2.3: What do you think, monks? Which is greater, the blood you have shed from having your heads cut off while transmigrating & wandering this long, long time, or the water in the four great oceans?”
sn15.13:2.4: “As we understand the Dhamma taught to us by the Blessed One, this is the greater: the blood we have shed from having our heads cut off while transmigrating & wandering this long, long time, not the water in the four great oceans.”
sn15.13:3.1: “Excellent, monks. Excellent. It is excellent that you thus understand the Dhamma taught by me.
sn15.13:3.2: “This is the greater: the blood you have shed from having your heads cut off while transmigrating & wandering this long, long time, not the water in the four great oceans.
sn15.13:3.3: “The blood you have shed when, being cows, you had your cow-heads cut off: Long has this been greater than the water in the four great oceans.
sn15.13:3.4: “The blood you have shed when, being water buffaloes, you had your water buffalo-heads cut off…
sn15.13:3.5: when, being rams, you had your ram-heads cut off…
sn15.13:3.6: when, being goats, you had your goat-heads cut off…
sn15.13:3.7: when, being deer, you had your deer-heads cut off…
sn15.13:3.8: when, being chickens, you had your chicken-heads cut off…
sn15.13:3.9: when, being pigs, you had your pig-heads cut off: Long has this been greater than the water in the four great oceans.
sn15.13:3.10: “The blood you have shed when, arrested as thieves plundering villages, you had your heads cut off…
sn15.13:3.11: when, arrested as highway thieves, you had your heads cut off…
sn15.13:3.12: when, arrested as adulterers, you had your heads cut off: Long has this been greater than the water in the four great oceans.
sn15.13:3.14: From an inconceivable beginning comes the wandering-on. A beginning point is not discernible, though beings hindered by ignorance and fettered by craving are transmigrating & wandering on. Long have you thus experienced stress, experienced pain, experienced loss, swelling the cemeteries—enough to become disenchanted with all fabrications, enough to become dispassionate,
sn15.13:4.1: That is what the Blessed One said.
sn15.13:4.2: Gratified, the monks delighted in the Blessed One’s words.
sn15.13:4.3: And while this explanation was being given, the minds of the thirty monks from Pāva—through lack of clinging—were released from effluents.
